</section>

<section class="page">
<div class="toprule"></div>
<div class="section-head"><div><div class="eyebrow">Authoritative rule variability</div><h2 style="margin-top:10px">The exact source matters more than the payer logo.</h2></div><p class="side">Examples below are educational and current to the cited 2026 sources. Always validate the live policy and contract before operational use.</p></div>
<table>
<thead><tr><th style="width:18%">Program / payer example</th><th style="width:23%">What the authoritative source says</th><th style="width:27%">Operational implication</th><th>Control lesson</th></tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr><td><b>Original Medicare</b></td><td>Submit to the correct Medicare Administrative Contractor no later than one calendar year after the date of service, subject to limited exceptions.</td><td>A general "12 months" label is incomplete unless the team confirms the correct contractor, date logic, and exception documentation.</td><td>Store the source, effective date, deadline basis, and exception rationale.</td></tr>
<tr><td><b>TRICARE</b></td><td>Claims in the United States and U.S. territories generally must be filed within one year of service; overseas claims generally have three years.</td><td>Location changes the filing window. A single 365-day statement does not cover every TRICARE scenario.</td><td>Capture service location and beneficiary context before calculating the deadline.</td></tr>
<tr><td><b>Cigna Healthcare</b></td><td>The public provider page lists 90 days for participating providers and 180 days for out-of-network providers, with stated exceptions.</td><td>Network participation and coordination-of-benefits circumstances may change the rule.</td><td>Exact network status and exception evidence are required fields.</td></tr>
<tr><td><b>UnitedHealthcare</b></td><td>The 2026 administrative guide directs providers to their internal contracting contact or Participation Agreement for timely-filing information.</td><td>A generic logo-based deadline may conflict with the controlling agreement.</td><td>Contract source hierarchy must be built into the workflow.</td></tr>
<tr><td><b>TennCare / MCO workflows</b></td><td>TennCare directs providers to applicable managed-care organization processes and has announced a future filing-limit change for certain claim categories effective July 1, 2027.</td><td>State Medicaid rules, MCO manuals, claim categories, and effective dates can change.</td><td>Policy monitoring and effective-date version control are essential.</td></tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<div class="grid3" style="margin-top:16px">
  <div class="card"><h3>Rule source</h3><p>Contract, provider manual, government manual, portal notice, or controlling law.</p></div>
  <div class="card black"><h3>Rule context</h3><p>Plan, product, network, claim type, date logic, state, and effective date.</p></div>
  <div class="card"><h3>Rule evidence</h3><p>Source title, version, retrieval date, screenshot or document reference, and reviewer.</p></div>
</div>
</section>

<section class="page">
<div class="toprule"></div>
<div class="eyebrow">Failure-origin map</div><h2 style="margin-top:10px">The denial appears at the end. Control is often lost much earlier.</h2>
<div class="timeline">
  <div class="tbox"><b>01</b><span>Service completed</span></div>
  <div class="tbox"><b>02</b><span>Charge captured</span></div>
  <div class="tbox"><b>03</b><span>Documentation complete</span></div>
  <div class="tbox"><b>04</b><span>Payer identified</span></div>
  <div class="tbox"><b>05</b><span>Rule verified</span></div>
  <div class="tbox"><b>06</b><span>Claim created</span></div>
  <div class="tbox"><b>07</b><span>Claim submitted</span></div>
  <div class="tbox"><b>08</b><span>Receipt accepted</span></div>
  <div class="tbox"><b>09</b><span>Rejection resolved</span></div>
  <div class="tbox"><b>10</b><span>Closure verified</span></div>
</div>
<table style="margin-top:18px">
<thead><tr><th>Failure point</th><th>Visible downstream problem</th><th>Why the deadline control failed</th><th>Patient / operational consequence</th></tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr><td>Incorrect product selected</td><td>Claim routed to wrong payer or address</td><td>The workflow used a payer brand instead of the exact plan and contract.</td><td>Delayed adjudication, avoidable rework, possible patient bill.</td></tr>
<tr><td>Documentation incomplete</td><td>Claim held in work queue</td><td>No aging threshold or escalation owner existed.</td><td>Deadline days consumed before the claim was even created.</td></tr>
<tr><td>Static deadline table</td><td>Incorrect filing date entered</td><td>Source version and effective date were not controlled.</td><td>False sense of readiness and missed filing window.</td></tr>
<tr><td>Transmission only</td><td>Rejected claim assumed filed</td><td>"Sent" was treated as "accepted" without acknowledgement review.</td><td>Claim never entered payer adjudication.</td></tr>
<tr><td>No proof of original filing</td><td>Late-filing dispute cannot be supported</td><td>Submission evidence was not stored with the claim record.</td><td>Contractual write-off or lost reimbursement exposure.</td></tr>
<tr><td>COB hold without reset logic</td><td>Secondary claim ages silently</td><td>Primary EOB date and secondary filing rule were not linked.</td><td>Patient confusion and delayed balance resolution.</td></tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<div class="blackbar" style="margin-top:18px"><strong>First loss of control</strong><p>The organization treated timely filing as a static payer lookup instead of a source-controlled, evidence-based, closed-loop workflow.</p></div>
</section>

<section class="page">
<div class="toprule"></div>
<div class="section-head"><div><div class="eyebrow">Simulated no-PHI case</div><h2 style="margin-top:10px">A 24-claim queue with hidden deadline exposure.</h2></div><p class="side">All values are synthetic and created for educational demonstration only.</p></div>
<p class="lead">A simulated specialty practice reviews a mixed-payer claim queue. Staff have a spreadsheet containing payer names and broad filing ranges, but the file does not record the exact product, contract source, effective date, claim type, acknowledgement status, or proof of original submission.</p>
<div class="kpis">
  <div class="kpi"><b>24</b><span>synthetic claims</span></div>
  <div class="kpi"><b>7</b><span>within 30 days</span></div>
  <div class="kpi"><b>5</b><span>missing rule source</span></div>
  <div class="kpi"><b>4</b><span>no receipt evidence</span></div>
